name: cuDF (NVIDIA RAPIDS)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); requires NVIDIA GPU; no API costs

auth.notes: Local GPU library; no auth required

performance.notes: GPU-accelerated operations 10-100x faster than pandas for large datasets (>1M rows); overhead at small scale

agent_readiness.notes: NVIDIA RAPIDS GPU-accelerated DataFrame library with pandas-compatible API. Subset of pandas operations implemented in CUDA. GPU memory management. Integration with cuML (GPU ML), cuGraph (GPU graphs), Dask for distributed. Zero-code-change acceleration via cudf.pandas mode. Excellent for large-scale data preprocessing in ML pipelines.

gotchas: NVIDIA GPU required — AMD/Apple Silicon not supported
Not all pandas operations implemented — gaps require CPU fallback
GPU memory limits constrain dataset size (80GB VRAM max per GPU)
CUDA version compatibility between cuDF, CUDA toolkit, and GPU drivers
best_when: Large-scale data preprocessing for ML training (>1M rows where pandas is slow)
Data science pipelines with NVIDIA GPU infrastructure already in place
Feature engineering pipelines before GPU-based model training (cuML)
Spark-like distributed processing on GPU clusters with Dask-cuDF
avoid_when: Small datasets where GPU overhead exceeds benefit
Operations requiring complete pandas compatibility (gaps exist)
summary: cuDF is NVIDIA RAPIDS' GPU-accelerated DataFrame library with a pandas-compatible API. Operations run on NVIDIA GPUs via CUDA, providing 10-100x speedup for large datasets (>1M rows). cudf.pandas mode enables zero-code-change acceleration. Integrates with cuML and Dask for distributed GPU data science. Requires NVIDIA GPU. Best for large-scale ML data preprocessing pipelines.
